steps.title: Apply the Zero Product Property
steps.content: When a product equals zero, at least one factor must be zero. The equation $4x(x-3)(x+9)=0$ is already factored. The factors are 4, $x$, $(x-3)$, and $(x+9)$. Since 4 is constant and nonzero, we solve $x=0$, $x-3=0$, and $x+9=0$.

steps.title: Solve each factor and identify the answer
steps.content:
Setting each remaining factor equal to zero:
$$x = 0$$
$$x - 3 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 3$$
$$x + 9 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -9$$
The three solutions are $x = 0, 3, -9$. Among the given choices, only $x = 0$ appears. That matches choice B — B is correct.
